report.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live; next steps are submission bundle parity and calibration.

research.root_cause: The perch cached-probe baseline (val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.665) is live but submission bundle parity is not yet validated. The immediate gap is not model performance but submission pipeline readiness: the notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline needs a CPU-only, internet-off bundle that reproduces the local score before any daily submission quota is consumed. The val_prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc (0.662) is close to the primary metric (0.665), confirming the probe head is working but calibration refinement remains conditional.
queries: perch cached probe submission bundle CPU offline parity
Bayesian prior calibration anchor submission readiness
class coverage gap fitted vs active classes perch probe
holdout soundscape validation metric interpretation cached probe
submission bar minimum iteration threshold perch baseline
adopt_now: [adopt] Validate submission bundle parity for run-0001-perch-baseline before consuming any daily submission quota — this is the single blocking task. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-submission-findings]

[adopt] Treat val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.665 as the authoritative local signal for this run. Do not over-index on soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.992 (train-side) or prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc=0.487 (unseen). [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-validation-and-metric-semantics, imports-kaggle-agent-00-experiment-rules-md-current-runtime-interpretation]

[adopt] Build the CPU-only, internet-off submission bundle now by packaging the Perch embedding cache + linear probe + Bayesian prior into the scored notebook format. Dry-run locally to confirm the bundle reproduces predictions. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-submission-findings]
consider: [consider] After bundle parity is confirmed, the next iteration should target prior calibration refinement — the val_prior_fusion score (0.662) suggests the Bayesian prior is functional but not optimal. Anchor-based calibration against historical LB scores could improve candidate ranking. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-submission-findings, policy: policy-prior-calibration]

[consider] If fitted_class_count < active_class_count in the probe head, expand class coverage before micro-tuning thresholds or temperature. This remains conditional until we inspect the probe output dimensions. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-03-next-experiment-priors-md-perch-probe-lane, policy: policy-class-coverage]

[consider] Embedding-head ensembles (adding a second complementary probe) could add signal, but only after the single-probe baseline submission is validated. Do not block on this. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings, policy: policy-probe-head]

[consider] Do not submit the perch baseline as a standalone first submission. Per the submission bar, iterate through calibration → ensemble → soundscape prior → post-processing before submitting. Current baseline is round 1. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-04-submission-bar-md-example]
reject: [reject] Do not consume daily submission quota until the CPU-only bundle has been dry-run locally and reproduces predictions within tolerance. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-submission-findings]

[reject] Do not pursue ornamental architecture changes or micro hyperparameter tuning on the probe head before submission bundle parity and class coverage are resolved. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-sed-findings, imports-kaggle-agent-research-run-0003-0002-perch-probe-round-3-close-8-class-coverage-gap-to-75-75-and-apply-calibration-md-actionable-guidance]

[reject] Do not trust the train-side soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.992 as a submission-readiness signal — it is not holdout-aware and will overstate quality. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-validation-and-metric-semantics]
positive_priors: backbone: Holdout validation is the most trustworthy local signal — val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.665 anchors this run. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-validation-and-metric-semantics, policy: policy-backbone]

prior_calibration: Submission is not just notebook packaging — candidate choice, anchor history, and slot usage all matter. Bundle must reproduce local predictions. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-submission-findings, policy: policy-prior-calibration]

preprocessing_aug: SED references are adoption checklists, not blocking changes. Highest-value gaps are frontend/training semantics. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-sed-findings, policy: policy-preprocessing-aug]

probe_head: Embedding-head models add complementary signal even when standalone scores are not best. Useful for future ensemble. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings]
negative_vetoes: class_coverage: Past regression from round 3 — always diff configs/code before changing. Do not make multiple changes at once. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-research-run-0003-0002-perch-probe-round-3-close-8-class-coverage-gap-to-75-75-and-apply-calibration-md-actionable-guidance, policy: policy-class-coverage]

submission_bar: Submitting a raw perch baseline without iteration is rejected per submission bar guidance. Current baseline is round 1 of the expected multi-round iteration cycle. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-04-submission-bar-md-example]
conditional_leads: class_coverage: If fitted classes lag active classes, expand coverage before micro-tuning. Conditional pending probe output inspection. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-03-next-experiment-priors-md-perch-probe-lane, policy: policy-class-coverage]

probe_head: For cached-probe runtime, holdout-aware soundscape validation is the primary signal; fall back to prior_fusion only if absent. [card: imports-kaggle-agent-00-experiment-rules-md-current-runtime-interpretation, policy: policy-probe-head]

claims.summary: For the current cached-probe runtime, treat holdout-aware soundscape validation as the intended local primary signal whenever it is produced.
Embedding-head style models can add complementary signal even when their standalone score is not the best.
If fitted classes lag active classes, expand coverage before micro-tuning.
Submission is not just notebook packaging.
Holdout validation is the most trustworthy local signal for iteration.
Immediate**: Revert to the run-0002 code state as the base for round 4. Diagnose the round 3 regression by diffing configs and code before making any new changes.
Public SED references are useful mainly as adoption checklists.

Does the current code produce a submission-ready CSV, or does the bundle need new inference glue code?
What is the expected LB score range given 0.665 local validation?

cards.title: Adopt Now
cards.summary: 1. **Validate submission bundle parity.** Package the Perch embedding cache + linear probe + Bayesian prior into a CPU-only, internet-off notebook. Dry-run locally before any Kaggle submission. (submission-findings)
cards.text:
1. **Validate submission bundle parity.** Package the Perch embedding cache + linear probe + Bayesian prior into a CPU-only, internet-off notebook. Dry-run locally before any Kaggle submission. (submission-findings)
2. **Anchor on val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc.** This is the holdout-aware primary signal. Ignore the inflated train-side 0.992. (validation-and-metric-semantics, experiment-rules)

cards.title: Actionable Guidance
cards.summary: Immediate**: Revert to the run-0002 code state as the base for round 4. Diagnose the round 3 regression by diffing configs and code before making any new changes. Close the 8-class gap with a single minimal change, th...
cards.text: **Immediate**: Revert to the run-0002 code state as the base for round 4. Diagnose the round 3 regression by diffing configs and code before making any new changes. Close the 8-class gap with a single minimal change, then validate that fitted_class_count reaches 75 before touching anything else.

cards.title: Consider (Post-Parity)
cards.summary: **Prior calibration refinement**: The 0.662 prior-fusion score vs 0.665 probe score suggests the Bayesian prior is close but not optimal. Anchor-based calibration against LB history could close this gap.
cards.text:
- **Prior calibration refinement**: The 0.662 prior-fusion score vs 0.665 probe score suggests the Bayesian prior is close but not optimal. Anchor-based calibration against LB history could close this gap.
- **Class coverage check**: If fitted classes lag active classes, expand coverage before threshold/temperature tuning. Conditional until probe output dimensions are inspected.
- **Embedding ensemble**: A second complementary probe head could add signal, but only after the single-probe baseline submission is validated.
- **Submission bar compliance**: The baseline is round 1. The submission bar guidance recommends iterating through calibration, ensemble, soundscape prior, and post-processing before submitting.

cards.title: Current State
cards.summary: The perch cached-probe baseline completed with **val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc = 0.665**. The run succeeded but the system correctly identifies that submission bundle parity is the blocking gap — not model iteration.
cards.text: The perch cached-probe baseline completed with **val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc = 0.665**. The run succeeded but the system correctly identifies that submission bundle parity is the blocking gap — not model iteration.

cards.title: Validation And Metric Semantics
cards.summary: Holdout validation is the most trustworthy local signal for iteration.
cards.text:
- Holdout validation is the most trustworthy local signal for iteration.
- Small soundscape validation subsets can be useful, but they are not a universal ranking metric across different training regimes.
- Historical reports repeatedly show that one metric can look weak while another better-matched split shows the model is actually strong.

cards.title: Perch And Embedding Findings
cards.summary: Embedding-head style models can add complementary signal even when their standalone score is not the best.
cards.text:
- Embedding-head style models can add complementary signal even when their standalone score is not the best.
- Joint use of focal and soundscape-derived information is valuable because it improves coverage and representation alignment across domains.
- When fitted class count is much lower than active class count, the next move should target coverage first, not just threshold tuning.

cards.title: Example
cards.text:
**Bad**:
- "Perch baseline" scores 0.75 → submit ❌
- Why: It's just cached embeddings + linear probe

**Good**:
- Round 1: "Perch baseline" → iterate
- Round 2: "Perch + calibration" → iterate
- Round 3: "Perch + ensemble" → iterate
- Round 4: "Perch + soundscape prior" → iterate
- Round 5: "Perch + post-processing" → iterate
- Round 6: "Perch full pipeline" → submit ✅
- Why: 6 rounds, multiple techniques, validated approach

capability_packs.title: Veto Checker
capability_packs.when_to_use: Use when deterministic policy rules and decision constraints should mechanically block weak branches.

capability_packs.title: Branch Diversifier
capability_packs.when_to_use: Use when the next batch should preserve sibling diversity instead of serially repeating one axis.
capability_packs.returns: Recommended branch mix and target branch count.

capability_packs.when_to_use: Use when branch selection should be constrained by typed axis, low-information patterns, and active envelope rules.
capability_packs.returns: Supported axis tags, low-information patterns, and active typing constraints.

capability_packs.title: Ledger Miner
capability_packs.when_to_use: Use when recent run history should bias the next frontier.
